briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware provider Synapse, highlighting custodial risks in embedded banking.

- **[Visa Threats Report: As Network Security Strengthens, Criminals Accelerate Shift to AI-Enabled Social Engineering](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/visa-threats-report-as-network-security-strengthens-criminals-accelerate-shift-to-ai-enabled-social-engineering)** — *Financial Post* — Visa's Spring 2026 biannual report warns that as payment network security improves, fraudsters are increasingly turning to AI-powered social engineering tactics to target consumers.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling Big Tech's growing interest in leveraging open banking data for financial services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in payments markets and challenging incumbent financial institutions.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[RBC to manage Canadian share orders for SpaceX's mega-IPO](https://financialpost.com/fp-finance/banking/rbc-goldman-sachs-spacex-ipo-banks)** — *Financial Post* — RBC has been tapped alongside Goldman Sachs to manage Canadian share orders for SpaceX's landmark IPO targeting a valuation of over US$2 trillion.

- **[Canada's annual inflation rate rose to 2.8% in April, thanks to soaring energy prices](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-inflation-april-2026-9.7203790?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's inflation climbed to 2.8% in April 2026, driven largely by surging energy costs, putting renewed pressure on household finances and lending conditions.

- **[OpenAI partners with 1Password to secure coding agent Codex](https://betakit.com/openai-partners-with-1password-to-secure-coding-agent-codex/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian password security company 1Password has partnered with OpenAI to provide credential and secrets management for its AI coding agent Codex.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a regulatory journey still resonating as implementation continues to evolve. *(Note: Original article from 2021; included for ongoing relevance to Canada's open banking policy context.)*

- **[Annapolis Royal municipal workers seek conciliation after bargaining reaches impasse](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/annapolis-royal-municipal-workers-seek-conciliation-after-bargaining-reaches-impasse)** — *Financial Post* — CUPE Local 3552 representing municipal workers in Annapolis Royal, Nova Scotia has filed for conciliation after collective bargaining broke down, a development with local financial and public service implications for Atlantic Canada.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A critical examination of the darker consequences of fintech's rapid growth, including financial exclusion and consumer harm — themes directly relevant to Atlantic Fintech's mission of building an inclusive and responsible ecosystem.

- **[Ghost brokers targeting 17 to 25 year-olds with fake car insurance online](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c1w29zw0q44o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — The UK's financial watchdog is warning that fraudulent insurance brokers are exploiting young consumers via social media, a cautionary tale for Canadian regulators and fintech players focused on consumer protection and financial inclusion.

- **[TV presenter says abusive ex gave her no access to her own money](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cgqpzg12p0yo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — A high-profile case of financial abuse highlights the critical importance of financial autonomy and inclusion — an area where Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintech innovators can play a meaningful role in supporting vulnerable populations.
